LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SHARON FENG'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS MAN HONG AO AND WEI OI CHE WERE REQUIRED TO SEEK LEAVE OF COURT BEFORE FILING THEIR ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION PAPERS FOCUS ONLY ON THE PURPORTED AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE OF STANDING AND FAIL TO EXPLAIN WHY THEY ALLEGED TWENTY-SEVEN OTHER AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES AND PURPORTEDLY RESERVED THE RIGHT TO ADD EVEN MORE. WHILE LEAVE TO AMEND AN ANSWER IS LIBERALLY GRANTED, IT MUST BE REQUESTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jul-31-2017 Judgment Entered

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication/ And Notice
Off Calendar Jun-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MAN AO and WEI CHE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is DENIED as to Defendant Man Hong Ao's motion for summary judgment and GRANTED as to defendant Wei Oi Che's motion for summary judgment. The failure to allege an affirmative defense of standing does not preclude Mr. Ao from obtaining summary judgment (McKinny v. Board of Trustees (1982) 31 Cal.3d 79, 90). Mr. Ao also satisfied his initial summary judgment burden by presenting evidence from Ms. Feng's deposition that Ms. Feng did not loan any of her own money to defendants and she did not have any interest in or receive any benefit from the loans. Moreover, Ms. Feng's deposition corrections and declaration contradicting her live deposition testimony are inadmissible per D'Amico v. Board of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal.3d 1, 21-22. (See generally EBC, Inc. v. Clark Building Systems, Inc. (3rd Cir. 2010) 618 F.3d 253, 270-71 (deposition corrections are treated the same as declarations for purposes of the federal rule analogous to the D'Amico rule); Hambleton Brothers Lumber Co. v. Balkin Enterprises, Inc. (9th Cir. 2005) 397 F.3d 1217, 1225-26 (same and relying on and quoting decisions from the Seventh and Tenth Circuits on the same point); see also Shapero v. Fliegel (1987) 191 Cal. App. 3d. 842, 849-850 (suggesting but not explicitly holding that deposition corrections are treated the same as declarations for purposes of the D'Amico rule)). Nonetheless, Ms. Feng created a triable issue on the "standing" issue by the declarations of Wong Chun Lao and Pou Kin Fong, which suffice to deny Mr. Ao's motion for summary judgment. Ms. Che is entitled to summary judgment because she presented evidence that she was not a party to the loan contracts and Ms. Feng has not presented any admissible evidence to the contrary. Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Defendants And Their Counsel Pursuant To Ccp 128.7
Off Calendar Jun-16-2017 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSEPH M. BREALL OF BREALL & BEALL LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-345-0545 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SHARON FENG'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Defendants Man Hong Ao and Wei OI Che were required to seek leave of court before filing their answer to first amended complaint. Defendants' opposition papers focus only on the purported affirmative defense of standing and fail to explain why they alleged twenty-seven other affirmative defenses and purportedly reserved the right to add even more. While leave to amend an answer is liberally granted, it must be requested. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk
